 {"id":"bd-11wv","title":"Bubbletea: track async event thread via TaskTracker","description":"# Bubbletea Async Event Thread Tracking

## Problem
In program.rs, the async event loop path (run_async / async_event_loop) spawns
an event polling thread at line ~1109:

    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

This thread uses a CancellationToken to know when to stop, but it is NOT
spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking(). The graceful shutdown sequence is:

1. cancel_token.cancel()          // signals all tracked tasks
2. task_tracker.close()           // prevents new tasks
3. task_tracker.wait() with 5s timeout  // waits for tracked tasks

Since the event thread is NOT tracked, step 3 doesn't wait for it. The thread
may still be blocked in event::poll() or event::read() when the async runtime
shuts down. This creates a race: the event thread may try to send on a dropped
tokio channel, causing a panic or silent failure.

## Why It Matters
- Graceful shutdown is incomplete — the event thread outlives the runtime
- In production SSH servers, accumulated leaked threads = resource exhaustion
- The 100ms poll timeout means the thread *usually* stops quickly, but "usually"
  is not "always" — event::read() can block indefinitely on some platforms

## Solution
Replace std::thread::spawn() with task_tracker.spawn_blocking() which:
- Registers the thread with TaskTracker
- Ensures graceful shutdown waits for it
- Proper cancellation via the existing CancellationToken

The spawn_blocking signature:
    task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

This is a minimal change — the thread body stays identical, only the spawn
mechanism changes.

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (async event loop, line ~1109)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event thread spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking()
- Graceful shutdown waits for event thread
- CancellationToken still used for early exit
- Existing tests pass","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.300659380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:27:18.235921068Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:27:18.235852150Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: bd-2353 (spawn_blocking implementation) and bd-2i18 (unit tests). Event thread now tracked by TaskTracker for proper graceful shutdown.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11wv","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when subtask is done:
1. bd-2353: Spawn via task_tracker.spawn_blocking
Plus the testing bead bd-2i18 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:26Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:
bd-2353 is a SINGLE LINE CHANGE:

Before: std::thread::spawn(move || { ... });
After:  task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

The task_tracker variable is already in scope at line 1110.

CRITICAL PATH: bd-2353 → bd-2i18 → bd-1azg","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:49:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11zs","title":"Property Tests: lipgloss dimension calculations","description":"Property-based tests for lipgloss width/height calculations.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify dimension calculation invariants
across all style combinations.

## Properties to Test

### Width Invariants
- [ ] output_width >= content_width (when width set)
- [ ] output_width == width when width > content_width
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_left + padding_right
- [ ] margin adds exactly margin_left + margin_right

### Height Invariants
- [ ] output_height >= content_lines
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_top + padding_bottom
- [ ] border adds exactly 2 (top + bottom) when present

### Unicode Width
- [ ] Wide chars (CJK) count as 2
- [ ] Combining chars count as 0
- [ ] Emoji width handled correctly
- [ ] ANSI sequences don't affect width

### Composition
- [ ] inherit() preserves dimension calculations
- [ ] copy() produces identical dimensions
- [ ] nested styles compute correctly

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn width_never_less_than_content(
        content in "[\\w\\s]{0,100}",
        width in 0usize..200,
        padding in 0usize..20,
    ) {
        let style = Style::new()
            .width(width)
            .padding_horizontal(padding);
        let output = style.render(&content);
        assert!(visual_width(&output) >= visual_width(&content));
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Dimensions always calculated correctly
- Unicode edge cases handled
- No panics for any style combination","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.723939917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659766118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659630014Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lipgloss","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1327","title":"Bubbletea: unit tests for sync thread lifecycle","description":"# Unit Tests for Sync Thread Lifecycle

## Purpose
Verify that the sync event loop properly captures JoinHandles and joins threads
on shutdown. These tests ensure the thread lifecycle fixes actually work.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_thread_handles_captured
Verify that all three spawned threads (external forwarder, input parser, command
executor) return JoinHandles that can be stored.

### 2. test_threads_exit_on_channel_drop
Verify that threads exit when their input channels are dropped.

### 3. test_shutdown_joins_all_threads
Integration test using a mock Program that spawns threads and verifies all
join within timeout.

### 4. test_join_timeout_logs_warning
Verify that if a thread doesnt join within timeout, a warning is logged.

## Logging Requirements
All tests should use tracing_test::traced_test to capture log output and
verify expected log messages appear.

## Key File
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (add tests to existing test module)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 test cases implemented and passing
- Tests use tracing_test for log verification
- Tests are deterministic (no flaky timing issues)
- Tests document expected behavior in comments","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:34.479166183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:18:57.750682406Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T01:18:57.750611073Z","close_reason":"Added 5 unit tests for thread lifecycle: test_thread_handles_captured, test_threads_exit_on_channel_drop, test_shutdown_joins_all_threads, test_thread_panic_handled_gracefully, test_external_forwarder_pattern. All tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-1dsp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-3azk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-1327","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NOTE: Test case 4 (test_join_timeout_logs_warning) should be renamed to test_slow_thread_join_logs_debug since we dont implement explicit timeout. The test verifies:
1. Thread joins eventually complete
2. Debug logging shows join progress
3. If a thread panics, warn logging shows the panic

Rename test case 4 to: test_thread_panic_logs_warning
Verify: match handle.join() logs Err case with tracing::warn","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:46:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1fxl","title":"Interactions: focus/blur awareness","description":"Use `FocusMsg`/`BlurMsg` to improve UX and avoid wasting resources when the terminal is unfocused.

Why:
- Focus reporting is an advanced terminal feature that bubbletea supports; the demo should prove it.
- Many TUIs burn CPU animating off-screen. We should behave like a polished app.

Behavior:
- Visual indicator:
  - show a subtle "unfocused" state in the header/status bar (icon + text)
  - reduce contrast of animated/active elements when unfocused
- Optional resource savings:
  - pause or throttle animations
  - pause or slow the background simulation tick

Constraints:
- Must not break determinism in tests:
  - focus/blur should be injectable as messages
  - headless runs should default to focused unless scenario changes it

Acceptance:
- App clearly communicates focus state.
- Unfocused mode avoids needless animation work without breaking the user’s mental model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:36.412081335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660400674Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:55:00.660271694Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","focus","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-114p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-2tl1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1fxl","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1i7e","title":"demo_showcase: CLI contract (modes, flags, help)","description":"Define the binary UX for launching the demo.

Proposed modes/flags (finalize based on UX concept):
- default: run interactive local TUI
- `--theme <name|auto>`
- `--theme-file <path>` (optional)
- `--seed <u64>` (stable demo data)
- `--no-animations`
- `--no-mouse`
- `--no-color` (force ASCII)
- `--no-alt-screen` (run without alt-screen; useful for debugging + demonstrating bubbletea println/printf)
- `--self-check` (headless CI-friendly render + exit)

Optional subcommands:
- `ssh --addr :2222 --host-key <path>` (wish integration; feature-gated)
- `export --html <path>` / `export --plain <path>` (optional: export snapshots)

Acceptance:
- `--help` is clean and self-explanatory.
- Flags map directly to `demo_showcase::Config` fields.
- Error messages are user-friendly (bad theme name, bad seed, missing file).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:30.810927082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539935175Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:34:48.539870655Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-33mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i7e","depends_on_id":"bd-szmb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1isw","title":"huh: Fix FilePicker single-file selection edge case","description":"# Bug: FilePicker Cannot Select Single File

## Problem Statement
In the FilePicker navigation logic, the down-arrow condition prevents
selecting the only file when there's exactly one file:

```rust
// Lines 3660-3663
if self.selected_index < self.files.len().saturating_sub(1) {
    self.selected_index += 1;
    // ...
}
```

When files.len() == 1:
- saturating_sub(1) = 0
- condition: 0 < 0 = FALSE
- navigation does nothing

## Root Cause Analysis
The condition `< len - 1` is meant to prevent going past the last item,
but it also prevents selecting the first (and only) item when starting
from index 0.

Actually, the bug is in the logic design. The condition should allow
moving TO the last item, not just items BEFORE the last.

Wait, re-reading: if selected_index is 0 and len is 1:
- Can't move down because 0 < 0 is false
But the item at index 0 IS selectable - it's already selected by default.

The real issue might be different - let me trace the full logic.

## Actual Issue
The condition prevents moving DOWN when already at last item, which is correct.
But if the initial state has selected_index pointing elsewhere or if there's
a reset that doesn't account for single-item lists, selection could be broken.

Let me re-examine:
```rust
// When len=1, saturating_sub(1)=0
// selected_index starts at 0 (presumably)
// Down key: 0 < 0 is false, no change (correct - already at end)
// Up key: 0 > 0 is false, no change (correct - already at start)
```

This is actually correct for navigation. The bug might be elsewhere -
perhaps in initialization or the actual selection/confirm logic.

## Further Investigation Needed
1. Check initial selected_index value for single-file directories
2. Check if Enter/confirm works with single file
3. Check if the file is visually highlighted

## Potential Fix Areas
If init doesn't set selected_index correctly:
```rust
fn set_files(&mut self, files: Vec<PathBuf>) {
    self.files = files;
    self.selected_index = 0.min(self.files.len().saturating_sub(1));
}
```

If confirm doesn't work:
```rust
fn confirm(&self) -> Option<PathBuf> {
    self.files.get(self.selected_index).cloned()
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn filepicker_single_file() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/single-file-dir");
    // Simulate single file
    picker.files = vec![PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")];
    picker.selected_index = 0;
    
    // Should be able to select it
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("only_file.txt"));
    assert!(/* file is highlighted */);
    
    // Confirm should return the file
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert_eq!(selected, Some(PathBuf::from("only_file.txt")));
}

#[test]
fn filepicker_empty_directory() {
    let mut picker = FilePicker::new("/empty-dir");
    picker.files = vec![];
    
    // Should handle gracefully
    let view = picker.view();
    assert!(view.contains("No files") || view.is_empty() == false);
    
    let selected = picker.confirm();
    assert!(selected.is_none());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single-file directories work correctly
- [ ] Empty directories handled gracefully
- [ ] Navigation works for 0, 1, and many files
- [ ] Selection/confirm works in all cases
- [ ] Tests added for edge cases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:05.943176512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:25:23.732034528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:25:23.731965359Z","close_reason":"Fixed FilePicker edge cases: empty files guard on down-nav and goto_bottom, height underflow protection with saturating_sub, added 15 comprehensive unit tests covering single-file, empty-directory, height=0/1, navigation bounds, selectability defaults, and format_size.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edge-case","filepicker","huh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1isw","depends_on_id":"bd-2xzk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2kfc","title":"Unit Tests: glamour markdown rendering","description":"Unit tests for glamour crate covering markdown parsing, theme application, word wrapping, and ANSI output.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightGorge","created_at":"2026-01-29T04:20:18.223399605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T04:31:55.551153998Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T04:31:55.551087414Z","close_reason":"Added 34 tests in markdown_edge_cases.rs covering API surface, all markdown elements, edge cases, style consistency, and determinism","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kfc","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kp1","title":"Settings: about/credits + diagnostics","description":"Add a polished About + Diagnostics section inside Settings.

Why:
- This is *real product* behavior (every serious ops tool has an About/Diagnostics panel).
- It doubles as a developer-facing debug surface and a user-facing confidence signal.

Diagnostics content (at minimum):
- demo_showcase version + build info (crate versions for bubbletea/lipgloss/bubbles/glamour/huh/harmonica/wish/charmed_log)
- active runtime Config (theme name, seed, toggles like mouse/animations/no-color/no-alt-screen)
- terminal info (detected color profile, TERM/COLORTERM, width/height last known)

Nice-to-have (if available cheaply):
- feature flags enabled (async, ssh, syntax)
- recent error/notification summary

Actions:
- "Copy diagnostics" (to clipboard if feasible, otherwise export to file)
- "Open diagnostics in pager" using the shell-out flow (bubbletea release/restore + sequence)

Acceptance:
- Diagnostics are readable, well-formatted, and update when Config/theme changes.
- Actions never hang in CI/headless mode; failures become toasts/logs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:09.931592707Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:40:48.116508915Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:40:48.116444074Z","close_reason":"Implemented About + Diagnostics section with version info, terminal info, feature flags, and export/pager actions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-194c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kp1","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2lkz","title":"Docs: in-doc search + match navigation","description":"Add search within the current Docs page (like a pager/reader).

Why:
- Search is a make-or-break capability for terminal docs viewers.
- It showcases textinput + highlight rendering + keybinding conventions.

Requirements:
- `/` enters search mode (focuses a query input).
- Highlights matches in the rendered doc output.
- `n`/`N` jumps to next/previous match.
- Show match count and current match index (e.g., "3/12").

Performance:
- Do not require an expensive full markdown re-render on every keystroke.
- Prefer searching within the already-rendered text buffer and applying highlight overlays.

Testability:
- The search/match navigation logic should be unit tested via the shared filtering/search engine tests.
- Covered by the Docs E2E scenario.

Acceptance:
- Feels like a real pager: fast, predictable, keyboard-centric.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:44.495821283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:20:08.701338676Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:20:08.701269868Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","search"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-22vc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-3jg2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2lkz","depends_on_id":"bd-i0i9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2m2l","title":"Harden wish tea middleware against BubbleTea thread join panics","description":"Replace spawn_blocking await unwrap in tea::middleware with graceful join-error handling (fatal session output + early return), and add regression test for panicking BubbleTea model.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:15:34.106101614Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:15:39.365749822Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2mvl","title":"Quality: snapshot tests for key views","description":"Add snapshot-style tests for key rendered views so UI regressions are caught early.

Why:
- TUIs are easy to break visually (alignment, borders, truncation) with small refactors.
- Snapshots complement state-based tests by asserting the actual rendered output.

What to snapshot (minimum):
- Dashboard (80x24)
- Jobs list/table (80x24)
- Logs (a few entries, follow mode on/off)
- Docs (one doc rendered at two widths)
- Settings (toggles + theme picker)

Snapshot strategy:
- Prefer ASCII/no-color snapshots for stability.
- If we snapshot colored output, normalize to deterministic sequences or snapshot via the "export plain" path.
- Keep snapshots small and focused (don’t snapshot megabytes of logs).

Acceptance:
- Snapshots are deterministic in CI.
- Failures are diff-friendly and point to the exact view that changed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:13:28.393184434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:23:44.113020382Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:23:44.112955111Z","close_reason":"Created tests/snapshot_tests.rs with 16 snapshot tests using insta. Covers Dashboard, Jobs, Docs, Settings, Services, Files, Wizard at various sizes (80x24, 120x40), plus help overlay, sidebar collapsed, and responsive layouts. Logs page excluded due to dynamic timestamps. Added insta to dev-dependencies.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","insta","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zeq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-3kk5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2mvl","depends_on_id":"bd-mwty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2myg","title":"Dashboard: live metrics + status indicators","description":"Wire dashboard widgets to the demo data model so the dashboard is *alive* (real-time updates, plausible trends, state changes).

Why:
- Static numbers look fake. Live metrics make the dashboard feel like a real ops tool.
- This also validates the simulation engine and the app’s tick/update architecture.

Requirements (minimum):
- Live-updating metrics:
  - requests/sec (or throughput)
  - p95 latency (or similar)
  - error rate
  - job throughput / queue depth
- Health/status indicators:
  - per-metric health state (ok/warn/error) derived from thresholds
  - trends (up/down/flat) based on deltas over a window
  - at least one periodic “incident” / degradation to prove the UI can surface issues
- Feedback:
  - state transitions produce a toast/alert + (optional) log entry
  - drill-down panel should explain *why* a metric is warn/error (thresholds, recent values)

Implementation notes:
- Metrics come from the deterministic simulation engine; use a seed in test/E2E.
- Avoid noisy/flappy states:
  - consider hysteresis or smoothing so warn/error doesn’t flicker on boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Values visibly change over time.
- Health transitions are obvious and explainable.
- No flicker/jank; dashboard remains responsive.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health state computation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend computation over a known sequence
  - “incident” generation is deterministic for a fixed seed
- E2E:
  - smoke tour sees changing values
  - settings/theme scenario confirms dashboard updates immediately after toggles.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:57.979377768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344449577Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:38.344380729Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2myg","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw3","title":"lipgloss: Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening","description":"# Epic: lipgloss Code Consolidation & ANSI Parsing Hardening

## Background
The lipgloss crate provides terminal styling (colors, borders, layout). During deep review,
code duplication and incomplete ANSI handling were identified as maintenance risks.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Three Independent visible_width() Implementations - HIGH
**Locations**:
- lib.rs lines 365-417: Full state machine with Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc states
- style.rs lines 1965-1996: Simplified two-state approach
- backend.rs lines 621-640: Even more simplified, no OSC handling

**Problem**: Each version has different completeness levels. They may produce different
results for the same input, causing layout inconsistencies.

**Risk**: Maintenance nightmare - bug fixes need to be applied to 3 places.

### 2. Incomplete OSC Sequence Handling - MEDIUM
**Location**: lib.rs lines 403-411
**Problem**: OSC sequence termination (ST = ESC \) is not properly validated.
The state machine transitions to Esc state on seeing ESC but doesn't verify
the following backslash character.

**Risk**: Malformed OSC sequences could cause incorrect width calculations.

### 3. Border Color Indexing Confusion - LOW
**Problem**: Border colors stored as [top, right, bottom, left] but accessed
inconsistently across different methods.

**Risk**: Wrong colors could be applied to wrong edges in edge cases.

## Design Philosophy
- Single Source of Truth: One canonical visible_width implementation
- Defensive Parsing: Handle all edge cases in ANSI parsing
- Clear Documentation: Document the expected format for all color arrays

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single visible_width() function used everywhere
- [ ] Complete ANSI escape sequence handling including OSC
- [ ] Consistent border color indexing throughout
- [ ] All changes have regression tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:16:49.723527312Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:01.232066002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:41:01.231998907Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-10h6 (visible_width consolidation, CLOSED) and bd-awkz (OSC sequence fix, CLOSED by WildSnow). lipgloss code consolidation and ANSI parsing hardening is done.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","code-quality","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw3","depends_on_id":"bd-gyzr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
